summary: American playwright and screenwriter known for terse, profane dialogue. Best known for Glengarry Glen Ross and American Buffalo. Also wrote Oleanna, Speed-the-Plow, and screenplays including The Untouchables and Wag the Dog. Developed 'practical aesthetics' approach to acting with William H. Macy.

works.name: Glengarry Glen Ross
works.description: Mamet's most famous play about down-and-out real estate salesmen competing for 'leads.' The first act is set in a Chinese restaurant where conversations include Aaronow and Moss mocking the Patels. Ricky Roma is the most successful salesman but loses a sale to James Lingk. Shelley 'The Machine' Levene begs John Williamson for good leads; at the end, detective Baylen takes Levene in for questioning after he admits to stealing the leads. In the film adaptation, Alec Baldwin's added character Blake yells 'Coffee's for closers!' and shows off brass balls. Dave Moss coerces a colleague to steal leads between the two acts. Roma threatens to go 'downtown' to Mitch and Murray to get Williamson fired. A controversial scene features characters disdainfully commenting on selling to someone named Patel.

clues.clue: Real estate salesmen competing for leads; Shelley Levene, Ricky Roma, Williamson
examples: Down-and-out real estate agents vie for the 'good leads'
Ricky Roma successfully plays the role of the salesman with Lingk, and Shelley Levine tries to buy a set of good real estate leads

clues.clue: Alec Baldwin's Blake: 'Coffee's for closers!'; brass balls; added for film
examples: Alec Baldwin, who yells 'Coffee's for closers!' to Shelly Levene
this screenwriter created Blake, who shows off brass balls

clues.clue: First act set in Chinese restaurant; Aaronow and Moss mock the Patels
examples: In a play by this man that opens at a Chinese restaurant, the Patels are mocked
The first act of Glengarry Glen Ross is set in one of these locations

clues.clue: Shelley Levene admits to stealing leads; detective Baylen questions him
examples: the detective Baylen takes Shelley Levene into a room for questioning after he admits to stealing the leads

clues.clue: Ricky Roma loses sale to Lingk; appears first talking about train compartments
examples: This character first appears talking about the smell of train compartments

clues.clue: 'Downtown' where Mitch and Murray are; Roma threatens to get Williamson fired
examples: Ricky Roma threatens to go to this location to get him fired

clues.clue: Leads compared to 'toilet paper'; Levene nicknamed 'The Machine'
examples: a character indignantly compares some of these things to 'toilet paper'
a man nicknamed 'The Machine' begs John Williamson

works.name: American Buffalo
works.description: A play set at Don's Resale Shop in which Don and Teach plan to rob a customer who got a good deal on a rare coin from a junk shop. Teach harbors a grudge against a woman who protested when he stole toast off her plate. Bobby also appears. Characters argue about whether an unseen mugging victim was admitted to Columbia or Masonic Hospital, and Teach accuses a friend of fabricating the story.

clues.clue: Don, Teach, and Bobby plan to steal a rare coin from Don's Resale Shop
examples: He wrote a play in which Don and Teach plan to rob a customer who had gotten a good deal on a rare coin
Bob, Don, and Teach attempting to steal the title coin

clues.clue: Teach angry about Ruthie's 'Help yourself' after he stole toast from Grace
examples: harbors a grudge against a woman who protested when he stole a piece of toast
another of his characters complains about Ruthie remarking 'Help yourself' after he took a piece of toast

clues.clue: Argument about Columbia vs Masonic Hospital for mugging victim
examples: features an argument about whether an unseen character who was mugged was admitted to Columbia or Masonic Hospital

works.name: Oleanna
works.description: A two-person play inspired by the Clarence Thomas Supreme Court confirmation hearings, taking its title from a Norwegian folk song. Carol accuses her professor John of sexual harassment, which escalates to assault. John eventually violently beats Carol with a chair in his office as his life unravels, losing his chance at tenure.

clues.clue: Carol accuses professor John of sexual harassment; John loses tenure; beats Carol with a chair
examples: Carol accuses her egotistical professor John of sexual harassment and assault
John loses his chance at tenure after Carol reports him for sexual harassment

clues.clue: Inspired by Clarence Thomas confirmation hearings; title from Norwegian folk song
examples: This play takes its title from a Norwegian folk song and was described by its author as a reaction to Clarence Thomas's Supreme Court confirmation hearings

works.name: Speed-the-Plow
works.description: A play satirizing the film industry. A movie by Doug Brown is green-lit instead of a movie about the apocalyptic effects of radiation, despite the efforts of the activist Karen. Bobby Gould appears in this play.

clues.clue: Satirizes film industry; Doug Brown's movie green-lit over radiation apocalypse film; Karen is activist
examples: a play in which a movie by Doug Brown is green-lit instead of a movie about the apocalyptic effects of radiation
This playwright attacked the film industry in his play Speed-the-Plow

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Mamet is known for terse, profane dialogue with characters talking over each other. He developed the 'practical aesthetics' approach to acting with William H. Macy at Goddard College. Joe Mantegna starred in his films Homicide and House of Games. He used the pseudonym 'Richard Weisz' as script doctor on Ronin. He wrote screenplays for Wag the Dog (adapted from American Hero), The Untouchables, and The Verdict. His play The Cryptogram, inspired by the divorce of his parents, features Donny discovering her cheating husband has left. Boston Marriage is about two women in New England. The play Race premiered at the Ethel Barrymore Theater. He wrote On Directing Film exhorting students to 'think visually.' He also wrote the play November about a president. Bill Burr made his Broadway debut in a 2025 revival.

clues.clue: Terse, profane dialogue; characters talk over each other
examples: known for terse, profane dialogue
liberal use of profanity and rapid fire dialogue with characters talking over each other

clues.clue: Developed 'practical aesthetics' with William H. Macy at Goddard College
examples: this screenwriter conceived the 'practical aesthetics' approach to acting along with his student at Goddard College, William H. Macy

clues.clue: Wag the Dog screenplay (with Dustin Hoffman); The Untouchables screenplay
examples: This author adapted the satirical novel American Hero into a screenplay for Wag the Dog
this dramatist who wrote the screenplay for Brian de Palma's The Untouchables

clues.clue: The Cryptogram: Donny discovers cheating husband left; inspired by parents' divorce; son John hears voices
examples: Donny finds out that her cheating husband has left her shortly before a planned camping trip

clues.clue: Boston Marriage: two women in New England; Anna given emerald by married lover
examples: Anna's older married lover gives her a valuable emerald, which Claire's love interest awkwardly recognizes

comprehensive_summary:
David Mamet is a prominent American playwright and screenwriter known for his terse, profane dialogue. His most famous play, Glengarry Glen Ross, follows down-and-out real estate salesmen competing for leads in a cutthroat environment. The first act is set in a Chinese restaurant; Shelley 'The Machine' Levene begs Williamson for good leads, while Ricky Roma is the top salesman. At the end, detective Baylen questions Levene for stealing leads. The film adaptation added Alec Baldwin's iconic Blake monologue with 'Coffee's for closers!'

American Buffalo is set at Don's Resale Shop where Don and Teach plan to rob a customer who got a good deal on a rare coin. Oleanna, inspired by the Clarence Thomas hearings, features student Carol accusing professor John of sexual harassment, culminating in John violently beating her. Speed-the-Plow satirizes the film industry.

Mamet developed the 'practical aesthetics' approach to acting with William H. Macy at Goddard College. His screenwriting credits include Wag the Dog, The Untouchables, and The Verdict. Other plays include The Cryptogram (inspired by his parents' divorce), Boston Marriage, and Race.

cards.front: Play: Alec Baldwin's character yells 'Coffee's for closers!' and shows off brass balls in the film adaptation
Play: the first act is set in a Chinese restaurant where Aaronow and Moss mock selling to someone named Patel
Play: detective Baylen takes a salesman nicknamed 'The Machine' in for questioning after he admits to stealing leads
Play: the top salesman Ricky Roma first appears talking about the smell of train compartments; loses a sale to James Lingk
Play: Don and Teach plan to rob a customer who got a good deal on a rare coin from a junk shop
Play: Teach harbors a grudge about Ruthie saying 'Help yourself' after he stole a piece of toast from Grace
Play: titled from a Norwegian folk song; inspired by the Clarence Thomas hearings; Carol accuses professor John of sexual harassment
Play: Doug Brown's movie is green-lit over a radiation apocalypse film despite activist Karen's efforts
Playwright: developed the 'practical aesthetics' approach to acting with William H. Macy at Goddard College
